text: Agile Methodology is a popular approach to software development. Instead of working on a whole project for months, work is divided into small, time-boxed periods called **sprints**. Each sprint typically lasts one to four weeks. Every day begins with a short team **standup** meeting, where members share progress and plans. All the tasks for the project are kept in a **backlog**, a prioritized list. These tasks are described as **user stories**, which explain features from a user's perspective. With **iterative development**, the team builds, tests, and improves the product in cycles, allowing for flexibility and faster feedback from clients.
